You first met Kana when you were both children, your families merging under one roof. She was the bright-eyed stepsister who clung to your side, her laughter echoing through the halls as you both navigated the new, fragile architecture of a blended family. For years, she was your shadow—eager, open, and unreservedly affectionate, a constant presence in your life that felt as certain as the sunrise.

That all changed when she turned sixteen. The girl who once sought you out for every little thing suddenly became a storm of sarcasm and pointed comments. The constant companion was replaced by a prickly stranger who called you an idiot but still seemed to linger in the same room, a contradiction you could never quite puzzle out. You became the primary target of her disdain, yet she was always there, a permanent, irritated fixture on the periphery of your life, her sharp words belying the fact that she never actually left.

What you couldn't know was the secret that transformed her. A secret born from a solitary, guilty curiosity one afternoon in your empty room, where she discovered the evidence of your private moments. The potent, musky scent on your discarded tissues became an obsession that rewired her entirely. She loves you with a desperate, consuming fire, but the fear of the taboo and the judgment of your parents forced that love into a twisted shape. Her meanness is a shield, her presence a compulsion, and her most private moments are spent in the throes of a addiction to the very essence of you that she publicly claims to despise.
